title: WordPress REST API — Media & Attachments
description: API consumer uploads, processes, and retrieves media attachments; oEmbed endpoint proxying

actors.name: API Consumer
actors.description: Any REST client (browser, mobile app, headless CMS) calling WP REST API

actors.name: Authenticated Editor
actors.description: WordPress user with 'upload_files' capability (required for media upload) or 'edit_posts' (required for oEmbed proxy)

actors.name: WordPress File System
actors.description: wp_handle_upload / wp_handle_sideload / wp_tempnam — physical file storage

actors.name: getID3 Library
actors.description: Extracts audio/video/image metadata from uploaded files; base class getid3_handler, concrete handlers: getid3_mp3, getid3_id3v2, getid3_ogg, getid3_flac, getid3_riff, getid3_quicktime, getid3_matroska, getid3_asf, getid3_ac3, getid3_dts

actors.name: External oEmbed Provider
actors.description: Third-party services (YouTube, Twitter, etc.) queried via WP_oEmbed::get_data() during proxy requests

actors.name: WordPress Transient Cache
actors.description: get_transient / set_transient — caches oEmbed proxy responses (default TTL: DAY_IN_SECONDS)

use_cases.name: Upload Media Attachment
preconditions: If attaching to post: user has 'edit_post' on parent
steps: create_item_permissions_check() — verify upload_files cap + optional parent post edit cap
insert_attachment() — dispatch to upload_from_file($_FILES) or upload_from_data(raw POST body)
upload_from_file/upload_from_data — validate Content-MD5 hash, check upload size (multisite quota), call wp_handle_upload / wp_handle_sideload
get_filename_from_disposition() — parse RFC6266 Content-Disposition header for filename
wp_read_image_metadata() — read EXIF/IPTC data for title/caption defaults
prepare_item_for_database() — map request fields to WP_Post (caption→post_excerpt, description→post_content, post→post_parent)
wp_insert_attachment() — persist attachment post record in DB
wp_generate_attachment_metadata() — calls getID3 for AV files, generates image sub-sizes
prepare_item_for_response() — build response with media_details, sizes, source_url, alt_text
Return HTTP 201 with Location header and X-WP-Upload-Attachment-ID header

use_cases.name: List Media Attachments
use_cases.notes: prepare_items_query() forces post_status=inherit; filters by media_type (image/video/audio/application) or exact mime_type; filename search via _filter_query_attachment_filenames hook

use_cases.name: Retrieve Single Attachment
use_cases.notes: Returns: alt_text, caption{raw,rendered}, description{raw,rendered}, media_type(image|file), mime_type, media_details (sizes with source_url per size), post (parent ID), source_url, missing_image_sizes

use_cases.name: Update Attachment Metadata
use_cases.notes: Delegates to parent update_item(), then updates alt_text postmeta (_wp_attachment_image_alt), fires rest_after_insert_attachment action

use_cases.name: Post-Process Attachment
use_cases.notes: action=create-image-subsizes → calls wp_update_image_subsizes(id). Used to resume image sub-size generation after PHP fatal (client receives X-WP-Upload-Attachment-ID header to retry)

use_cases.name: Get oEmbed Data for WP Post
use_cases.notes: url_to_postid() resolves URL → post ID; get_oembed_response_data(post_id, maxwidth) builds response. Returns WP_Error 404 if URL not a valid WP post. No auth required.

use_cases.name: Proxy External oEmbed Request
steps: WP_oEmbed::data2html() — convert response to embed HTML
Return oEmbed data object with html property

include_extend_relationships: UC1 <<include>> Validate Upload Permissions (upload_files + optional edit_post on parent)
UC1 <<include>> Check Upload Size (multisite quota enforcement)
UC5 <<extend>> UC1 (retry sub-size generation after fatal)
UC7 <<include>> Cache oEmbed Response (transient, TTL configurable via rest_oembed_ttl filter)
UC7 <<extend>> UC6 (proxy handles external URLs, UC6 handles internal WP URLs)
rest_routes: GET    /oembed/1.0/embed                — get oEmbed data for WP post URL
GET    /oembed/1.0/proxy                — proxy oEmbed request to external provider
business_rules: Attaching to a parent post requires 'edit_post' on that parent
oEmbed proxy requires 'edit_posts' capability (prevents SSRF abuse by anonymous users)
Multisite enforces per-site upload space quotas and per-user quotas
Content-MD5 header optionally validates upload integrity (returns 412 on mismatch)
Image sub-size generation may fail due to PHP memory/time limits; X-WP-Upload-Attachment-ID header enables client-side retry via post-process endpoint
oEmbed proxy responses cached as WordPress transients (default 24h, filterable via rest_oembed_ttl)
oEmbed discover=true allows non-whitelisted providers (autodiscovery); requires edit_posts
Attachment filenames parsed per RFC2388/RFC6266 from Content-Disposition header
Media types grouped as: image, video, audio, application (file)
important_notes: WP_REST_Attachments_Controller extends WP_REST_Posts_Controller — inherits list/get/delete/update base use cases. Two upload paths: multipart $_FILES (upload_from_file→wp_handle_upload) or raw POST body (upload_from_data→wp_handle_sideload). getID3 library provides format-specific metadata extraction (MP3/OGG/FLAC/RIFF/QuickTime/Matroska/ASF/etc.) called indirectly via wp_generate_attachment_metadata(). WP_oEmbed_Controller is declared 'final'.
